fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oregon Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity oregon, how to transfer audiologist license to oregon, audiologist compact oregon, oregon audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Oregon Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology
board.mailing_address: Oregon Health Licensing Office, PO Box 14470, Salem, OR 97309-5056
board.physical_address: 800 NE Oregon St., Suite 407, Portland, OR 97232

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series administered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Oregon.

steps.title: Prepare Required Documents
steps.description: Gather official transcripts verifying a doctoral degree in Audiology (Au.D., Ph.D., Sc.D., or equivalent) from a regionally accredited institution and proof of passing the Praxis Examination in Audiology (5041 or 5030 for previous versions).

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register and create a new account on the Oregon Health Licensing Office's (HLO) Applicant and Licensee Portal to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the HLO Applicant and Licensee Portal, select the Audiologist application type, and accurately complete all required sections, uploading your official transcripts and Praxis examination scores.

steps.title: Submit Fingerprints for Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the HLO Applicant and Licensee Portal to complete the mandatory criminal background check via fingerprinting, ensuring compliance with state requirements.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $150 through the HLO Applicant and Licensee Portal. The initial license fee of $100 will be due upon approval of your license.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: Your complete application will first undergo an initial review by HLO staff (typically within 10 business days), followed by formal review and approval by the Oregon Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology during their quarterly meetings.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oregon audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oregon have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Oregon?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Oregon
hero.dek: Audiologists in Oregon must apply for initial licensure through the Oregon Health Licensing Office's (HLO) Applicant and Licensee Portal.

official_links.portal_name: Oregon Health Licensing Office's (HLO) Applicant and Licensee Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the HLO Applicant and Licensee Portal, click 'Register Here' (or 'Register Now'), and follow the prompts to create an account by providing personal information and setting up a username and password.
